title: I Stood Before the Open Fridge
paragraphs: I opened the fridge and just stood there.
It was almost empty. I knew that already.
But I looked anyway. For a long minute.
I was not really hungry. I was tired.
Tired of choosing things, all day long.
What to wear, what to read, what to buy.
The cold light felt strangely calm.
I closed the door and chose nothing at all.
translation: 냉장고를 열고 그냥 거기 서 있었어요.
거의 비어 있었어요. 이미 알고 있었어요.
그래도 들여다봤어요. 긴 1분 동안요.
사실 배고프지 않았어요. 피곤했어요.
하루 종일 무언가를 고르는 게 피곤했어요.
뭘 입을지, 뭘 읽을지, 뭘 살지요.
차가운 불빛이 이상하게 차분했어요.
문을 닫고 아무것도 고르지 않았어요.

quiz.question: What did the writer choose in the end?

title: I Stood Before the Open Fridge
paragraphs: I opened the refrigerator door and simply stood there in front of it.
It was almost completely empty, and I already knew that perfectly well.
But I kept looking inside anyway, for one long and strangely peaceful minute.
I was not actually hungry at all; the real truth was that I was tired.
I was tired of choosing things, one after another, all day long.
What to wear, what to read, what to watch, what to buy and eat.
The cold electric light of the fridge felt oddly, unexpectedly calming.
In the end, I gently closed the door and chose nothing at all.
translation: 냉장고 문을 열고 그 앞에 그냥 서 있었어요.
거의 완전히 비어 있었고, 저는 이미 그걸 아주 잘 알고 있었어요.
그래도 어쨌든 계속 들여다봤어요. 길고 이상하게 평화로운 1분 동안요.
사실 전혀 배고프지 않았어요. 진짜 진실은 제가 피곤했다는 거예요.
하루 종일, 하나하나, 무언가를 고르는 게 피곤했어요.
뭘 입을지, 뭘 읽을지, 뭘 볼지, 뭘 사서 먹을지요.
냉장고의 차가운 전등 불빛이 이상하게, 뜻밖에 마음을 가라앉혔어요.
결국, 저는 문을 살며시 닫고 아무것도 고르지 않았어요.

quiz.question: How long did the writer keep looking?
What was the writer truly tired of?

title: I Stood Before the Open Fridge
paragraphs: I opened the refrigerator door and simply stood there motionless in front of it.
It was almost completely empty, a fact I already knew perfectly and entirely well.
But I kept gazing inside anyway, for one long and strangely peaceful, suspended minute.
I was not actually hungry in the slightest; the quiet truth was that I was simply tired.
I was worn out from choosing things, one small decision after another, all day long.
What to wear, what to read, what to watch, what to buy, what to answer, what to eat.
The cold electric light of the empty fridge felt oddly and unexpectedly calming to me.
In the end, I gently closed the door and, for once, chose absolutely nothing at all.
translation: 냉장고 문을 열고 그 앞에 그냥 꼼짝 않고 서 있었어요.
거의 완전히 비어 있었어요. 제가 이미 완벽히, 온전히 알던 사실이요.
그래도 어쨌든 계속 들여다봤어요. 길고 이상하게 평화로운, 멈춘 듯한 1분 동안요.
사실 조금도 배고프지 않았어요. 조용한 진실은 그냥 제가 피곤했다는 거예요.
하루 종일, 작은 결정 하나에 또 하나, 무언가를 고르느라 지쳐 있었어요.
뭘 입을지, 뭘 읽을지, 뭘 볼지, 뭘 살지, 뭐라고 답할지, 뭘 먹을지요.
텅 빈 냉장고의 차가운 전등 불빛이 제게 이상하고 뜻밖에 마음을 가라앉혔어요.
결국, 저는 문을 살며시 닫고, 한 번만큼은, 정말 아무것도 고르지 않았어요.

quiz.question: How did the writer stand in front of the fridge?
quiz.questionKo: 글쓴이는 냉장고 앞에 어떻게 서 있었나요?

quiz.question: How did the cold light feel?

title: I Stood Before the Open Fridge
paragraphs: I opened the refrigerator door and simply stood there, motionless, in front of its faint glow.
It was almost completely empty, a plain fact I already knew perfectly and entirely well.
But I kept gazing inside anyway, for one long and strangely peaceful, suspended minute.
I was not actually hungry in the slightest; the quiet, inconvenient truth was that I was simply exhausted.
I was worn thin from choosing things, one small relentless decision after another, all day long.
What to wear, what to read, what to watch, what to buy, what to answer, what to finally eat.
The cold electric light of the empty fridge felt oddly, almost therapeutically calming to me.
In the end, I gently closed the door and, for once in a long while, chose absolutely nothing at all.
translation: 냉장고 문을 열고 그 희미한 불빛 앞에, 꼼짝 않고, 그냥 서 있었어요.
거의 완전히 비어 있었어요. 제가 이미 완벽히, 온전히 알던 명백한 사실이요.
그래도 어쨌든 계속 들여다봤어요. 길고 이상하게 평화로운, 멈춘 듯한 1분 동안요.
사실 조금도 배고프지 않았어요. 조용하고 불편한 진실은 그냥 제가 기진맥진했다는 거예요.
하루 종일, 작고 끈질긴 결정 하나에 또 하나, 무언가를 고르느라 닳아 있었어요.
뭘 입을지, 뭘 읽을지, 뭘 볼지, 뭘 살지, 뭐라고 답할지, 마침내 뭘 먹을지요.
텅 빈 냉장고의 차가운 전등 불빛이 제게 이상하게, 거의 치유하듯 마음을 가라앉혔어요.
결국, 저는 문을 살며시 닫고, 오랜만에 한 번만큼은, 정말 아무것도 고르지 않았어요.

quiz.question: How did the cold light feel, almost?

title: I Stood Before the Open Fridge
paragraphs: I opened the refrigerator door and simply stood there, motionless, bathed in its faint and indifferent glow.
It was almost entirely empty, a plain fact I already knew perfectly and comprehensively well.
But I kept gazing inside regardless, for one long and strangely peaceful, suspended minute of nothing.
I was not actually hungry in the slightest; the quiet, inconvenient truth was that I was simply depleted.
I was worn thin from choosing things, one small relentless decision after another, throughout the entire day.
What to wear, what to read, what to watch, what to buy, what to answer, what to finally bring myself to eat.
The cold electric light of the empty fridge felt oddly, almost therapeutically calming to my tired mind.
In the end, I gently closed the door and, for once in a very long while, chose absolutely nothing at all.
translation: 냉장고 문을 열고, 그 희미하고 무심한 불빛에 잠긴 채, 꼼짝 않고 그냥 서 있었어요.
거의 완전히 비어 있었어요. 제가 이미 완벽히, 포괄적으로 알던 명백한 사실이요.
그래도 상관없이 계속 들여다봤어요. 길고 이상하게 평화로운, 아무것도 없는 멈춘 듯한 1분 동안요.
사실 조금도 배고프지 않았어요. 조용하고 불편한 진실은 그냥 제가 소진됐다는 거예요.
하루 내내, 작고 끈질긴 결정 하나에 또 하나, 무언가를 고르느라 닳아 있었어요.
뭘 입을지, 뭘 읽을지, 뭘 볼지, 뭘 살지, 뭐라고 답할지, 마침내 뭘 먹을 마음을 낼지요.
텅 빈 냉장고의 차가운 전등 불빛이 지친 제 마음에 이상하게, 거의 치유하듯 마음을 가라앉혔어요.
결국, 저는 문을 살며시 닫고, 아주 오랜만에 한 번만큼은, 정말 아무것도 고르지 않았어요.

quiz.question: How is the fridge's glow described?
What was the writer, in the quiet truth?
quiz.questionKo: 조용한 진실 속에서 글쓴이는 어떤 상태였나요?

title: I Stood Before the Open Fridge
paragraphs: I opened the refrigerator door and simply stood there, motionless, bathed in its faint and wholly indifferent glow.
It was almost entirely empty, a plain and unembellished fact I already knew perfectly and comprehensively well.
But I kept gazing inside regardless, for one long and strangely peaceful, suspended minute of deliberate nothing.
I was not actually hungry in the slightest; the quiet, inconvenient truth was that I was simply and thoroughly depleted.
I was worn thin from choosing things, one small relentless decision after another, ceaselessly throughout the entire day.
What to wear, what to read, what to watch, what to buy, what to answer, what to finally summon the will to eat.
The cold electric light of the empty fridge felt oddly, almost therapeutically calming to my over-solicited mind.
In the end, I gently closed the door and, for once in a very long while, deliberately chose absolutely nothing at all.
translation: 냉장고 문을 열고, 그 희미하고 온전히 무심한 불빛에 잠긴 채, 꼼짝 않고 그냥 서 있었어요.
거의 완전히 비어 있었어요. 제가 이미 완벽히, 포괄적으로 알던 꾸밈없는 명백한 사실이요.
그래도 상관없이 계속 들여다봤어요. 길고 이상하게 평화로운, 의도적인 무(無)의 멈춘 듯한 1분 동안요.
사실 조금도 배고프지 않았어요. 조용하고 불편한 진실은 그냥 제가 완전히 소진됐다는 거예요.
하루 내내, 작고 끈질긴 결정 하나에 또 하나, 쉼 없이 무언가를 고르느라 닳아 있었어요.
뭘 입을지, 뭘 읽을지, 뭘 볼지, 뭘 살지, 뭐라고 답할지, 마침내 뭘 먹을 의지를 끌어낼지요.
텅 빈 냉장고의 차가운 전등 불빛이 지나치게 시달린 제 마음에 이상하게, 거의 치유하듯 마음을 가라앉혔어요.
결국, 저는 문을 살며시 닫고, 아주 오랜만에 한 번만큼은, 의도적으로 정말 아무것도 고르지 않았어요.

quiz.question: What kind of "nothing" was the suspended minute?
How is the writer's mind described?
